title: MapleStory N Beginner's Guide
author: MapleStory N
summary: The official gameplay curriculum for getting started, character progression, items and equipment, monsters and dungeons, social systems, and account or service features in MapleStory N.

sections.title: Getting started
items: Choose a Basic or Secondary key layout, then customize key bindings, quickslots, macros, NPC interaction, and item pickup.
Open Maple Guide with F1 to see level-appropriate quests, theme dungeons, hunting grounds, bosses, and special content.
Quick Move is available when you are within 10 levels of relevant content and has a 30-second cooldown.

sections.title: Character progression
items: Learn skill basics, job advancement, 5th Job skills, and the systems used to strengthen a character.
Link Skills can be transferred from level 70 and reach level 2 at level 120.
Up to 12 Link Skills can be transferred; transfers can be changed up to five times per day and reset at 00:00 UTC.

sections.title: Combat and content
items: Use hunting fields, Runes, monsters, dungeons, and boss content as the main combat progression path.
Review boss requirements and Pure Boss Essence information before committing resources.
Party and Guild systems support group content and social progression.

sections.title: Items and service systems
items: The guide covers equipment, enhancement, inventory, currencies, and item-related systems.
Account, wallet, launcher, and marketplace topics are separated into official service documentation.
Use the linked official pages when a value or limit may have changed after a game update.

notes: Original MPStorys structured overview verified against the official MapleStory N documentation tree on 2026-07-18.
steps: Configure controls and quickslots, then open Maple Guide with F1.
Follow level-appropriate quests, theme dungeons, and hunting grounds.
Advance jobs and review Skill, Link Skill, and 5th Job progression systems.
Use Runes, dungeons, bosses, parties, and guilds as they become available.
Check the official item, equipment, and service documentation before spending limited resources.
